daily_fact: An electric-green stream flowing through Brooklyn’s Green-Wood Cemetery is a positive sign of spring, caused by harmless fluorescent dye that helps staff trace and maintain the historic cemetery’s water system.
topline_md: New York’s biggest updates center on affordability and public infrastructure: the City Council backed fully subsidized Fair Fares for 1.3 million riders, while officials also advanced plans on sidewalk-shed reform, highway expansion limits, and air-quality upgrades even as gaps persist in funding for volunteer fire companies and trash containerization.

civics_politics.title: Civics & Politics
civics_politics.summary_md:
### Civics & Politics

- Council backs expanding Fair Fares to fully cover subway and bus fares for 1.3 million New Yorkers. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixgFBVV95cUxQU1BSbGJseTF3bzcxZlo4VFpjcHVjTUlDWUNjVkRWUnc5X2dLY2J5SndoYURjbWNxSWROQXNmbjZqeVZSQXBpdDNmRUdwWmJpZ19vYi1qM0NTTWR1dDYySEhTSUZYZ2lBcGJIOVhvR0Vva1o1bl9Fbko0SHNZRnJRenVDQTctUjhJZnFxdDlyWU1iTXlKTEVIdHlBOW5vQjY5QnlFN0JZQ183Q180Z1gzeVVma0JtSGczU2oyS3hkTVFFLWxtZ0E?oc=5)
- City unveils a plan to reduce long-standing sidewalk sheds and falling-debris hazards across New York City. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- A state bill would block highway expansions near vulnerable communities, affecting future road projects in and around the city. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxOVXlkQ3FYVy1jamY0c2lZanN5ZVozNFJvck5JRDNqNUpKdlJHcXkxMjdYRzlpaTdXaXJjSm1fRWZsSTA3U3pEZ0gwdFY3b0dqbUhXOGc2MFB5QVZnaHoxSlI4Nk01dHR0Q1U1WkczdEg3RDJiNGdmMjdmRF9XMDJkallaMGgwY2EzdnhleDJhallQNVcySGVfY0dRMTBpZzdMdS1tancwQ3V2TnM?oc=5)
- AOC is urging Hochul to drop plans to widen the Cross Bronx Expressway. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqAFBVV95cUxORl9IOUhkby1UVUQ3R3JDcUNuOEUzc0kwTGxMMm5UcnJ5N3VXamZITjdzMzJmRk9QX2JDZ0hsOUUxdXpMUzZSX2R2THRxOGVrYmRMZzJFVVZKR0ZDdWZHZGxBQzV5eFhZd1ZscmFzY1hGTTRONUttYjBLRC1mMkhSbGRKbE1LVEwzRUZTZEN3LW1ic1hpSFJYVXpnb2puXzhVbmUtdXZDLWc?oc=5)
- A new bill would give City Council members more control over appointing local community board members. - Manhattan Chinatown is getting private-carting reforms aimed at overhauling commercial trash collection. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMioAFBVV95cUxNZFo3UkNZMTNkbnVaOXZFakZXNzRvenpublJyZ1QzTFFkYXAwTTFuWWt0ZjlMSWNvdGR4LW1Oc1BoUEhRRnpYaUdMNmtLTG1nRHVOOXlTcUw2VW9UQnQ1SmViQXpMV1FrYXowTVBvd0dMYXZad3RVQzdPNjhNY0tyNDJ0c1ZtbVRJTVRwdEpNc2NianBucEg4U05ic0FBR0l2?oc=5)

- Business and good-government groups are opposing a state bill requiring two-person subway train crews. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifkFVX3lxTFAwSmdMRVFOWXp4QmVuRDBtU3pWWlhMMlZPNThpQ1ZaV1phOFpwalRqVVJPTkdtR2RRVnFsMFBmNHZKNXlWbDV5NUJQS0VWQzVydG1aamFCaHpOWlJfRm9tdTdWWXZnNHVYVUlubWVjOC1qcW9waWtYZkZybEZFUQ?oc=5)
